issues.comment: When `evt.destinationCalendar` is `null`, `undefined`, or an empty array `[]`, the destructuring `const [mainHostDestinationCalendar] = evt.destinationCalendar ?? []` yields `mainHostDestinationCalendar = undefined`. Accessing `.integration` on `undefined` throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'integration')`. The old code used `evt.destinationCalendar?.integration` with optional chaining. The new code drops the `?.`, so any event with no destination calendar that uses Google Meet location will crash this handler. Fix: `mainHostDestinationCalendar?.integration !== "google_calendar"`

In both `updateEvent` and `deleteEvent`, the else branch of the ternary searches for `cal.externalId === externalCalendarId` but `externalCalendarId` is guaranteed falsy at that point. No calendar will ever have `externalId === undefined`, so the `find` always returns `undefined`. The old code fell back to `event.destinationCalendar?.externalId`. The new code always produces `undefined` in the fallback path, meaning the calendar ID is lost. For `deleteEvent` (where `externalCalendarId` is optional), this means events may fail to delete from the correct calendar. Fix: The else branch should find the appropriate calendar from the array differently, e.g., using `event.destinationCalendar?.[0]?.externalId` or matching on `credentialId`.

The original logic set `slug` directly when billing is disabled (`!IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED`) and `requestedSlug` in metadata when billing is enabled. The new code inverts the first condition: `...(IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED ? { slug } : {})` now sets `slug` when billing IS enabled. Combined with the second line that still sets `requestedSlug` when billing is enabled, the result is: Billing enabled: Both `slug` AND `requestedSlug` are set (wrong — slug should only be set after payment). Billing disabled: Neither `slug` nor `requestedSlug` is set (wrong — org has no slug at all). This breaks organization creation in both modes.

When neither the event type nor the organizer has a destination calendar, `destinationCalendar` is set to `null`. Later, `evt.destinationCalendar?.push(...teamDestinationCalendars)` is silently skipped because `null` doesn't have `push`. This means for COLLECTIVE team events, team members' destination calendars are dropped when the organizer hasn't set up a destination calendar. The default should be `[]` (empty array) instead of `null` so team calendars can still be pushed.
